//: E15 slice 5 — Lighthouse mobile performance budget, enforced in CI.
The bar is the v2 requirement (NFR-1 / M-18-02): mobile LCP under 2.5s on
throttled 4G. Visitors reach this app standing in a ferry queue on a phone,
often on a weak signal, so the mobile-throttled number is the real one.
Runs against a LOCAL production build, never the live site: a CI job that
measures production would fail on someone else's traffic spike and would
block a PR for a reason the author cannot act on.
numberOfRuns 3 + median aggregation because Lighthouse is noisy in CI.
Flakiness is handled by taking the median, NOT by loosening the budget.

settings.//: NO desktop preset — it would override the mobile emulation and throttling below, and we would be measuring a laptop while calling it a phone budget.

//: TWO KINDS OF THRESHOLD live here, and the distinction is deliberate.
STRICT — requirements the app already MEETS. Keep them tight so they
cannot silently regress: total-blocking-time, cumulative-layout-shift
and the JS transfer budget all passed the first CI measurement.
FLOORS — requirements the app does NOT yet fully meet. Ratcheted as
real work lands; never raised to let a slow change pass. History:
performance  target 0.85 (NFR / M-18-02)
0.72  first CI run measured ~0.79 across all four pages
0.85  E15-5c — now MET. See LCP note; floor is the target itself.
largest-contentful-paint  target 2500ms (NFR-1)
6000  first CI run measured ~5.4s across all four pages
4300  E15-5c: the <body> topo texture was a 445 KiB raw JPEG
(54% of home transfer, ~2.2s at the 4G rate above) tiled on
every route — the shared cost that pinned LCP uniformly near
5.4s regardless of page content. Re-encoded to 42 KiB WebP
(-91%, visually identical flat linework), LCP dropped to a
~3.5-3.8s median. 4300 sits above today's worst MEDIAN
(~3819ms) with headroom for median-aggregated CI noise.
STILL ABOVE the 2500 target: the residual ~1s is Lantern
simulating ~30 shell requests serialized over HTTP/1.1's 6
connections (the local standalone server speaks h1; prod is
h2 and multiplexes, so this lab number is pessimistic). No
non-structural lever remains; closing to 2500 needs owner
sign-off on request-count / transport work (epic forbids
structural refactors without it). THIS DOES NOT YET MEET NFR-1.
